yah pānī us baptismā kī taraf iśārā hai jo is vaqt āp ko najāt dilātā hai. is se jism kī gandagī dūr nahī̃ kī jātī balki baptismā lete vaqt ham xudā se arz karte ha͠i ki voh hamārā zamīr pāk-sāf kar de. phir yah āp ko yesu masīh ke jī uṭhne se najāt dilātā hai.

def: s.m. Choosing, selecting; choice, election, wish, request, boon, favour, blessing, good; anything chosen as a present; gift, reward, recompense; one who chooses, chooser; one who solicits a girl in marriage, suitor, wooer, lover; bridegroom, son-in-law; husband, lord, master; — breadth (of cloth); — adj. more: n. m. S. वर from वृ to choose. 1. A gift from heaven; a boon; blessing; supernatural power. 2. A bridegroom; husband. 1. Kannyā peṭ meṅ, ḍhūṅḍte phireṅ bar . Prov. The child in the womb, search for a bridegroom. 2. Aj kal bārah baras kī kannyā apne muṅh se bar māṅgtī hai . Prov. Girls only twelve years old for husbands ask with tongues so bold. 3. Do tiryoṅ ne kīyā siṅgār, Ban-ṭhan-ke woh bhaīṅ taīyār, Unke purakh ne aisī kīnī, Ek hī bar ko donoṅ dīnī . Riddle. Ans. Jūtī A pair of shoes. Two females decked and turned out fair, To one spouse wedded were the pair. 3. Master; lord; owner; head. Dekhi Rām chhabī kou ek kahe, Yog Jānkī yeh bar ahe . Rāmāyan. bār-jog , adj. Marriageable (a girl); arrived at puberty. bar-dān , n. m. 1. A wedding gift to a bride from her betrothed. 2. The answer to a prayer addressed to a saint or God.

(dakh.) To exasperate ( Harris ).

def: s.f. Fence, hedge, outer boundary or enclosure (of any kind); line, border, rim, margin, edge; edge (of a weapon or tool = S. पालि); line or row (of soldiers); front, frout rank; — (in comp.), A suffix signifying 'place,' enclosure, &c. ( = wār)
more: s. f. Edge (of a sword, knife, &c.), verge, edge, margin, a fence, a hedge, a line (of soldiers); name of a place near Monger. باڙاڙانا bāṛ uṛānā, To fire a volley. باڙباندهنا bāṛ bāndhnā, To enclose a field with thorns. باڙپکڙانا bāṛ pakṛānā, To encourage, to teach. باڙجهاڙنا bār jhāṛnā, To fire a volley. باڙچروانا bāṛ chirwānā, or باڙچيرنا bāṛ chīrnā, To sharpen. باڙدلوانا bāṛ dil-wānā, or باڙدينا bāṛ denā, To sharpen, to grind; to excite, to instigate, animate. باڙرکهنا bāṛ rakhnā, To sharpen.
subs: bāṛ bāṅdhnā (meṅ), To fence, enclose, hedge in
bāṛ-par ćaṛhānā (-ko), To encourage, instigate, incite, excite
(-par), bāṛ ćīrnā, bāṛ denā, bāṛ dilwānā, bāṛ rakhnā (-par), To put an edge (on), give an edge (to), to sharpen, whet
v.t. To blunt or notch the edge (of a sword, &c.)
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit वाट (vāṭa).

def: s.m. Perquisite of the ahīr or cowherd in milk (generally the milk of every eighth day).
adj. Great, large, excessive, &c.; (much used in comp. e.g.), baṛ-bolā, s.m. Noisy, talkative person; boaster, braggart
subs: adj. & s.m. Very fortunate; — one who is very fortunate
adj. & s.m. Big-bellied, pot-bellied; greedy, gluttonous; avaricious; — one who has a big belly; a greedy person, glutton
adj. Having large teeth; (in Bot.) sinuate
adj. & s.m. Having long ears; — long-eared man
adj. & s.m. Having a large nose; — a man with a big nose
adj. & s.m. Large-lipped, thick-lipped; — thick-lipped man.

def: rain; rainy season, monsoon
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian باران (bārān).

def: s.m. The process of wire-drawing (through the jantrī).
adj. Twelve ( = bārah, q.v.)
subs: s.f. lit. 'Twelve-fold force (zor)'; force, violence, compulsion; — adv. By force; — bārā-jorī karnā, To use force, bear strongly upon, to press, constrain, compel

s.f. (in Gram.), The aggregate of the twelve short vowels of the Nāgarī alphabet in combination with consonants (e.g. ka, kā, ki, kī, &c.); a kind of song, every line of which begins with a consonant.

def: s.m. Enclosed space, enclosure ( = gherā), area; cemetery; — fence, hedge ( = bāṛ); alms, charity (in this sense prob. connected with varaṇam)
more: s. m. 1. An enclosure. 2. Alms, charity.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Large, great, big, vast, immense, huge; grand, noble, high, exalted, eminent; grown up, old, senior, elder; superior, supreme, principal; grave, serious; vital, essential, important
more: adj. Large, great, big, greater, senior, elder, eldest, principal, &c. بڙارستاپکڙنا baṛā rastā pakaṛnā, To die. بڙاکرنا baṛā karnā, 1. To enlarge, to exalt, to promote. 2. To extinguish, to put out (a lamp, &c. as to use the direct phrase is deemed unlucky). بڙي پيٿ والاهونا baṛe peṭ wālā honā, To be patient.
s. (dakh.) Pulse or dāl and flour with aromaticks and spices made into balls and fried in ghī ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: s.m. Uncle; father-in-law; (slang) an astute fellow, consummate knave
s.m. Great or rich man; man of eminence or rank, grandee
s.m. Superior officer, head of a department
s.m. (Math.) The greatest common measure (syn. maqsūm-ilaih ʻāz̤am)
s.m. A kind of appraisement; money borrowed at a very high interest
s.m. High rank or station; dignity, consequence, importance, distinction: baṛā din, s.m. Great, or long, day; Christmas day
v.n. lit. 'To take the long journey or road'; to die
s.m. Great man; chief civil functionary; a resident (at court)
s.m. Large workshop, or establishment; extensive business; — baṛā karnā or kar-denā, v.t. To enlarge, increase, augment, lengthen; to exalt, promote; to extinguish (a light = baṛhānā)
s.m. Large house or building; grand house; great, rich, or noble house or family
s.m. Great name or reputation; — baṛā nām karnā, v.n. To acquire a great name, win a great reputation, become famous
(obl. pl. of baṛā), the great; grown up people, &c. (see baṛe); — baṛoṅ-meṅ milnā, v.n. To be grown up; to associate with elders or seniors, or with the great
bare-buṛhe, baṛe-log, s.m. pl. Grown-up people, elders, seniors, superiors; the great, the eminent; forefathers, ancestors
s.m. Great man, person of high position or rank; — s.f. High appointment; high position
s.f. Great matter, important thing or affair; difficult affair; — baṛī bāt huʼī, A great thing has been accomplished; — intj. thank Heaven!
s.f. Elder son's wife; — a wife who is older than her husband
s.f. A form of address to an elderly or respectable woman
s.m. Old man; — master of the house; — intj. Aged sir! reverend sir!
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀟𑁆𑀠 (vaḍḍha), from Sanskrit वृद्ध (vṛddha). Compare to Assamese বড় (bor), Bengali বড় (boṛ), Marathi बडा (baḍā), and Punjabi وَڈّا (vaḍḍā).

def: s.m. Condiment made of ground pulse (dāl, mūng, or māsh), formed into lumps and fried in ghī, butter, or oil (cf. baṛī).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀟𑁆𑀠 (vaḍḍha), from Sanskrit वृद्ध (vṛddha). Compare to Assamese বড় (bor), Bengali বড় (boṛ), Marathi बडा (baḍā), and Punjabi وَڈّا (vaḍḍā).

def: Christmas
wety: Compound of بَڑا (baṛā) + دِن (din), literally “big day or the great day”. Compare Bengali বড়দিন (boṛodin).

def: A market where foreign goods (especially smuggled items) are available, named after a place called Bāṛā in the northern tribal areas of Pakistan, which was originally famous among buyers for such goods.
s.m. Drawing water for irrigation; the song sung whilst drawing water; the man who stands over the well to tilt the leathern bucket (moṭ or ćaraṣ) when it is drawn up.
= bālā, q.v. s.f. Female child, girl, young woman (not more than sixteen years of age); woman; — small cardamoms; a medicinal and fragrant plant, Hibiscus tortuosus (cf. bāl-ćhaṛ).
s.m. Perquisite of the ahīr or cowherd in milk (generally the milk of every eighth day).
more: W. n. m. S. वार् water. 1. The act of drawing water from the well for irrigation, and also the song sung at the time. Merā bārā lāīyo re, Rām manāīyo re . Song. Bārā . * Met . A capacious mind. My bucket full of water rise, in Ram my hope of good all lies. 2. The man who stands over the well to turn over the leathern bucket ( moṭ or charas ) when drawn up. 3. The process of drawing wire through the jantrī . 4. Rus. A feast given on the death of some old man ( kāj ).

n. m. S वट् to surround. 1. An enclosure ( gherā ); circuit; area; arena ( daṅgal ). 2. Mah. A cemetry; churchyard. Kahāṅ lāī hai, wāh rī taqdīr, Muā bāṛe kā jis tarah se faqīr . Shauq. My blessed fate! what has it brought me to! This man now dead does beg and cling to me so. 3. Alms or charity dispensed at a Hin. marriage.
s. m. A boy: see بالا bālā.
1. ( वात ) s. m. (dakh.) Wind, air: see بيار bayār. 2. adj. Twelve: see باره bārah.

def: s.m. Time, turn; regard, respect
subs: (-ke), In respect of, concerning (e.g. kis-ke bāre-meṅ, In respect of whom?).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑀸𑀭𑀲 (bārasa), from Sanskrit द्वादश (dvā́daśa).

def: = بڙا baṛā, q.v. s.m. Perquisite of the ahīr or cowherd in milk (generally the milk of every eighth day).
more: Brij. n. m. S. वट or वड़ा. Cakes made of pulse-meal, and fried in butter or ghī , but commonly in oil. 1. Baṛe kaṛhāī meṅ tale jāte haiṅ . Mazāq. Jest. Cakes (your grand relations) we fry in oil. 2. Khān baṛā, khān-posh baṛā, khol-ke dekhā to ādhā baṛā . Prov. Large tray, large cover; but half a cake you soon discover.

adj. S. वड्ग great, or वृध्द increased. 1. Great; big; large; spacious; vast; immense; huge; enormous. 2. Bulky; voluminous; stupendous. 3. Grand; noble; august; heroic; magnanimous. 4. High ( ūṅchā ); lofty ( buland ); sublime. 5. Exalted; elevated; eminent; high-minded. 6. Meritorious; worthy; admirable. 7. Respectable; honorable; supreme; eminent. 8. Principal; highest; supreme; paramount. 9. Rich; wealthy; affluent; opulent. 10. Superior; main; prime; primary. 11. Senior; elder; major; better. 12. Old; eldest; chief; head; first. 13. Grave; serious; capital. 14. Vital; essential; important; material. baṛā abbā , Mah. n. m. 1. An uncle ( tāyā ); a father-in-law ( susrā ). 2. ( Slang ). A consummate knave ( bāvā ). baṛā ādmī , n. m. A great or rich man; a man of rank; a grandee. baṛā adh-kaṭ . (Math.) Major co-ordinate. baṛā af'sar , n. m. Superior officer; the head of a department. ba…
1. (for वटक ) s. m. Cakes made of ground pulse fried in butter or ghī. 2. (for بڙا) adj. Large, great.

def: s.m. Lamb; kid; fawn; the sign Aries.
more: s. m. A lamb, a kid. ¶ ba-rah, In or on the way.

def: s.m. Wind, &c. = bayār, q.v. s.f. Wind, air.

ety: for A. برآءة, v.n. fr. برٲ, cf. barī
def: s.f. A writing conferring immunity or exemption; commission, warrant, decree, assignment, letter; draught, cheque; — the fourteenth day of the month Shaʻbān, &c. (see shab-ě-barāt).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi برات (brat /⁠barāt⁠/) (c. 1693), from Sanskrit वरयात्रा (varayātrā), a compound of वर (vará, “suitor, bridegroom, husband”) + यात्रा (yā́trā, “journey”), literally “the groom's journey [to the bride]”.

def: adv. = bār-bār, and bārambār, q.q.v. s.f. Muttering, murmuring, grumbling, chattering; talking (in sleep), raving (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To mutter, &c. = baṛbaṛānā, and baṛṛānā.

def: v. n. 1. To coincide; fit; cover (a space); balance (an account). 2. To be equal, equivalent, on a par with; to balance. Sāl bhar meṅ sakhī, sūm, barābar ho jāte haiṅ . Prov. At the end of the year the spendthrift and the niggard are all one. 3. To be level, even, etc. 4. To mate; fellow; match; pair. Oṛhī chādar, huī barābar, maiṅ bhī shah kī khālā hūṅ! Prov. A shawl put on, her place beside the bridegroom's aunt! 5. To keep pace with; keep up with; to be a tie. 6. To come up to; overtake; reach to. 7. To be quits, even with. Dīyā līyā barābar huā . 8. To terminate; expire; come to an end; to come to nought; to be spent or over. Sārā rupayā barābar ho gayā . 9. To be choked, filled up, filled to the brim. Gaṛhā barābar ho gayā . 10. To be effaced, rubbed out, erased.

v. a. See uṛānā (11, 12, 15). 1. To make like; confirm; equalize; assimilate. 2. To balance; to be of equal weight. 3. To make level; plane; smooth. Gaṛhā barābar kar do . 4. To level; raze to the ground; destroy. Aṅgrezoṅ ne ādhī Dillī barābar kar dī . 5. To spend; exhaust; get to the end of. Sārā rupayā bārābar kar dīyā . 6. To efface; rub out; leave no trace. 7. To liquidate or discharge (a debt); repay. Likkhā paṛhā barābar kar dīyā . 8. To finish; complete; bring to an end. Āj sārā kām barābar kar dīyā. barābar kī ṭakkar, yā choṭ, yā joṛ , n. f. 1. ( Mech .) Equal forces working in opposite directions. 2. Men of equal rank, wealth, or power, mental or physical; equal shareholders. 1. Woh to barabar kī ṭakkar ko beṭī deṅgī . 2. Ādhe kā sājhī, barābār kī choṭ . Prov. A half share, an equal share. barābar kāṅṭe , adj. Of equal weight; equally balanced. barābar ke hisse , n. m. Equal shares. barābar-wālā , n. m. An equal.

s.m. Equal, peer, compeer; — adv. Regularly, uniformly, invariably, constantly; precisely, exactly; approximately; along with, together, side by side; conjointly, concurrently; straight on, continuously, without break or intermission, directly, immediately, without delay
more: adj. 1. Equal (in quality or quantity); as good as; neither more nor less. Phr. As broad as it is long; six of one and half a dozen of the other. 1. Bīmār kī rāt pahāṛ barabar . Prov. The sick man's night is as a mountain. 2. Coinciding; fitting. 3. Exact; precise; true ( ṭhīk ). 4. Level; flat; even ( chauras ). 5. At equal distance; parallel; horizontal. 6. Straight ( sīdhā ); direct; immediate. 7. Uniform; alike ( iksāṅ ); similar; resembling. 8. On a level with; balanced; on a par. 9. Of the same age ( ham-umr ). 10. Answering; corresponding; befitting; matching. 11. Convertible; interchangeable; equivalent. 12. Invariable; without difference; unchangeable. 13. Regular; arranged ( tartīb-wār ); orderly. 14. Just; exact; proportionate; commensurate. 15. Impartial; equitable; just; fair. 16. Indifferent; uninterested; unconcerned ( alag ). 17. Futile; ineffective; without result ( be-a…

adv. 1. Regularly; constantly ( hameshā ); always ( sadā ); uniformly; incessantly; again and again ( bār bār ); without exception. 2. Invariably; without variation or change. 3. Precisely; exactly; proportionably. 4. Adjacent to; near; approximately. 5. Concurrently; jointly; conjointly; together. 6. By the side of; along with; side by side; in a line ( barābar barābar ). 7. Directly; immediately; at once; without delay.
prep. Next; nigh; by; next door. barābar barābar . Emph. form of barābar . 1. In a line, row, or file. 2. Side by side; breast to breast; shoulder to shoulder.

adj. Abreast, even, level, plain, uniform, smooth, like, alike, up to, opposite, over against, equal, on a par, adequate, exact, accurate, straight. برابرآنا bar-ā-bar ānā, To overtake. برابرسرابر bar-ā-bar sarābar, adj. Equal, alike. برابرکرنا bar-ā-bar karnā, To smooth, compose, adjust, accord, assimilate, level, match. برابرهونا bar-ā-bar honā, To terminate, allow, equal, suit, agree.
adj. Correspondent, suitable. برابرکرنا bar-ā-bar karnā, To adapt.
subs: (-ke), To come on a level (with), come up (to), to overtake
adv. (emphat. of barābar), Breast to breast, shoulder to shoulder, side by side; in a row or line
adj. (f. -ī), Of the same size, weight, age, or standing, &c.; equal

v.t. To make level, plane, or smooth; to level, raze, demolish, destroy; to efface, rub or wipe out; to run through, waste, spend (as a fortune); to bring to a termination, complete, finish (a work); to liquidate or discharge (a debt); to make equal, like, or similar, to equalize, assimilate, balance, accord, match, adapt, compose, adjust

barābar-kī ćoṭ, s.f. Person of equal standing, rank, wealth or power; equal, peer; suitable match, partner or mate; equal shareholders; (in Mech.), equal forces acting in opposite directions; — barābar-kī ṭakkar-kā, adj. Of equal standing, rank, &c.; equally good, on a par, equal

v.n. To be level, even, smooth; to be levelled, razed, demolished; to be effaced, erased, wiped out; to be equal (to), on a par (with); to suit, fit, match, agree, coincide (with), be enough (for); to balance; to come abreast (of), come up (to), overtake ( = barābar ānā); to keep pace (with), keep up (with); to be complete; to be full, be up to the brim (as a hole); to terminate, come to an end, be expended, spent, run through, wasted; to be settled, adjusted, or discharged (as an account or debt); to be even (with), be quits.
wety: From Classical Persian برابر (barābar).

def: s.f. Level, par, equality, equalization, equilibrium, equipoise, balance; exactness, agreement, correspondence; parallelism; competition, emulation, rivalry; presumption, insolence
more: n. f. 1. Par; equality; equalization. 2. Balance; equilibrium; equipoise. 3. Correspondence; agreement; conformity; parallelism. 4. Competition; rivalry; emulation. Barābrī pe martā hai . 5. Insolence; check; presumption. barā'barī, yā barābrī kar'nā , v. n. 1. To cope or strive with; to vie. 2. To imitate; copy; do like. Tumhārī barābarī woh kare jo ṭāṅg uṭhā-ke mūte . ( Kuttā ) Prov. 3. To presume; to be insolent, presumptuous. 4. To withstand; emulate; rival. Merī kyā majāl hai jo āpkī barabarī karūṅ . 5. To go against; defy; challenge. 6. To bully; bear down; outstare.
s. f. Equality, exactness, emulation, rivalry, competition. برابري کرنا bar-ā-bar-ī karnā, To resist, oppose, vie, withstand, emulate, rival, equal.
subs: (-kī), To equal; ṭo try to equal; to copy, imitate; to vie (with), emulate, rival; to cope or strive (with); to confront, withstand, oppose, resist, defy; to be insolent or presumptuous.

def: s.m. Bull, ox, bullock ( = balad).
more: s. m. A bull, a bullock.

def: adj. Conferring a boon; granting a request or prayer; favourable, propitious (see bar, 'boon').

ety: old P. burādar; Pehl. brāt; Zend brātar; S. भ्रातर
def: s.m. Brother; relation, relative, kinsman
more: n. m. Z. birāṭar ; Pahlavī. birād ; S. भ्राता H. bhāī , 1. A brother ( bīr ). 2. Relation; relative; kindred.
s. m. Brother. برادرپرور birādar-parwar, Kind to friends and relations.
subs: s.m. Step-brother; uterine brother (syn. gailaṛ bhāʼī)
adj. Kind to relations, or to brethren and friends
s.m. Own brother, full brother (syn. sagā bhāʼī)
s.f. Calling oneself a brother, profession of fraternal affection

def: adj. & adv. Brotherly, fraternal; like a brother, becoming a brother.
more: adj. Brotherly; fraternal; like a brother. birādar-i-toam , n. m. H. juṛvāṅ bhāī . Twin brothers.
adj. or adv. Brotherly, like a brother.

def: s. f. A summer house. See باره دري bārah-dar-ī, under باره.
s.f. Brotherhood, fraternity, connection, relationship (syn. bhāʼī-ćārā); relation, relative, kindred, kinsfolk; community, society; band of musicians (called tāshe-wāle); — adj. Brotherly, fraternal
more: (from برادر) 1. s. f. Brotherhood, fraternity, relationship, company. 2. adj. Brotherly, fraternal.
subs: s.m. One who is put out of caste, outcast
v.t. To expel from a fraternity; to put out of caste; to excommunicate.

def: v.t. To cover or bull (a cow); — v.n. To be bulled (a cow).
more: v. n. To bull a cow.

def: adv. For the sake of, for ( = liye); — on account of, because of, by reason of; for the purpose of, in order to
more: n. f. S. वड्रता 1. Greatness; bigness; largeness; vastness. 2. Bulk; size; magnitude; dimensions; volume. 3. The adult state; advanced age; senility. 4. Superiority ( buzurgī ); excellence; importance; merit; preeminence. 5. Rank; grandeur; dignity. 6. Respect; honor; reverence. 1. Baṛe kī baṛāī, na chhoṭe kī chhuṭāī . Prov. Nor reverence for superiors nor condescension to inferiors. 2. Apnī baṛāī apne hāth hai . Prov. Saying. Every man's honor is in his own keeping. 7. Self-praise; boast; vaunt; brag ( shekhī ). Hameshā apnī baṛāī kīyā kartā hai. baṛāī denā , v. a. To show respect; do honor to ( ādar k .). baṛāī kar'nā, yā mār'nā , v. n. 1. To praise ( astutī k .); admire; applaud. 2. To boast; vaunt; brag; talk big; trumpet; show off. Lohā kare apnī baṛāī, ham bhī haiṅ Mahādeo ke bhāī . Prov. The iron vaunts himself sky high—"a brother of the trident god am I."
subs: adj. & adv. Nominal, ostensible, pretended, fictitious; — nominally, ostensibly, for the mere name of, in pretence or show.

def: adv. For; for the sake of; on account of; in order to; because of; in respect of; by reason of; concerning; on.

Hin. adv. For God's sake; for mercy's sake. Merā thā, so terā huā; barāė Khudā ṭuk dekhan de . Wom. Prov. He was mine, he is yours; for God's sake one brief look vouchsafe. (The mother's appeal to her daughter-in-law).
adj. Ped. for H. nām chār ko . Nominal; fictitious; not real or essential. barāë nām , adv. Nominally; ostensibly.
adj. Lighted up, kindled, set on fire, inflamed.
more: (part. pass.) adj. Inflamed, set on fire.

def: adv. With intense desire; with avidity, eagerly.

s.m. Hog, boar; the third or boar-incarnation of Vishṇu (in which he raised the earth from the bottom of the sea with his tusks); the earth or land so raised; land next to a village (see barāh and varāh).
more: n. m. S. वराह a hog, a boar. 1. The third incarnation of Vishnu. 2. Land next to a village, or more properly, the earth from its having been raised from the deep by Varaha.
s. m. A hog. See براه वराह .

def: adj. Twelve; — bārahoṅ, coll. n. The whole twelve, the twelve
subs: adj. lit. 'Twelve roads'; scattered, dispersed, cast to the winds, ruined, destroyed; embarrassed, bewildered, perplexed, distracted, confounded; at sixes and sevens, in confusion; at variance, in a state of discord; — bārah-bāṭ karnā, v.t. To scatter, disperse, throw into confusion, confound; to ruin, destroy; — bārah bāṭ honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be scattered, &c., to become houseless, ruined, &c., to be a vagabond; to be abandoned
adj. & s.m. Generally praised or approved, pure, perfect, genuine, good; sound, hale, hearty, well, convalescent; — the most refined, the purest (gold, water, &c.)
s.m. The twelve pillars which mark the boundary of a cantonment or encampment; the cantonment or encampment so bounded
adj. & s.m. Having twelve arches; — a bridge with twelve arches
bārah-dwārī, s.f. lit. 'Having twelve doors'; a summerhouse (generally in a garden)
s.m. lit. 'Having twelve tines'; a stag, antelope (?)

adj. & s.m. Pertaining to the twelve months of the year; — Hindī verse of twelve stanzas, corresponding to the twelve months of the year, and descriptive of the pain of separation from a husband, as also of the characteristic changes of the season, the scenery, and the pastimes which distinguish each month
adv. The whole twelve months, all the year round, at all times, always, perpetually
s.f. The twelve days of Moham mad's fatal illness.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑀸𑀭𑀲 (bārasa), from Sanskrit द्वादश (dvā́daśa).

def: s.m. Boar, &c. = bārāh, q.v. s.m. Hog, boar; the third or boar-incarnation of Vishṇu (in which he raised the earth from the bottom of the sea with his tusks); the earth or land so raised; land next to a village (see barāh and varāh).
more: n. m. A boar. See براه
s. m. 1. A boar. 2. The third avatār or incarnation of Vish̤ṇu in the form of a boar.

def: adj. Twelve ways. 1. Scattered; dispersed; cast to the winds ( tittar bittar ). 2. Not unanimous; disagreeing; at variance; torn by internal factions. Bārah bāṭ, aṭhārah paiṅḍe . Prov. Twelve roads, and eighteen foot paths. (He is embarrassed which way to take). 3. Confounded; perplexed; distracted; at a loss. Dhoban dhove kāpṛā re, woh Jhājham ke ghāt, Sāban machhlī le gaī re, dhoban bārah bāṭ . Kabīr. The clothes to wash the laundress to the water went, The fish went off with the soap—a sad predicament. 4. Useless; unprofitable; vain. Naī javānī bārah bāṭ, Kabhī na khāyā maṭṭhā bhāt . E. Prov. In vain his youth all passed away! ah cruel lot! A mess of rice and butter-milk he never got! 5. Ruined; destroyed; desolate. bārah bāṭ karnā , v. a. 1. To ruin; destroy. 2. To scatter; disperse; cast to the winds. bārah bāṭ hoṇā , v. n. 1. To be scattered. See bārah bāṭ. Āë ekī des se utre ekī ghāṭ, Hawā lagī sansār kī, ho gaë bārah bāṭ . Kabīr. All from one country come, alighted also at one port, By winds of worldly passions driven, all scattered are in sport. 2. To be abandoned, destitute of name or character. Bāp ke marte hī laṛke bārah bāṭ ho gaë .
n. f. 1. Twelve nations or powers of Europe, so called from the different hats or turbans peculiar to each. 2. Any council of twelve wise men.

s.m. Land next to, or surrounding, a village ( = bārāh, q.v.). s.m. Hog, boar; the third or boar-incarnation of Vishṇu (in which he raised the earth from the bottom of the sea with his tusks); the earth or land so raised; land next to a village (see barāh and varāh).

adj. Twelve. باره باٿ bārah bāṭ honā, To be vagabond, ruined or harassed. باه دري bārah-dar-ī (from باره and در a door; q. d. Having twelve doors) s. f. A summer house.

adj. 1. Perfect; accomplished. Us bin mujh ko chain na āwe, Woh merī tis ān bujhāwe, Hai woh sab gun bārah-bānī, "E. sakhī, sājan"? "Nā, sakhī, pānī ." Riddle. Without him I no rest can get, My thirst he quenches when we're met, "His rare perfections him endear." A lover, dear? "No, water, dear." 2. Restored to perfect health; in sound health. 3. Without flaw; unalloyed. — ho'nā To recover; to be restored to perfect health. bārā bachche-wālī , n. f. lit . A mother of twelve. def: adj. Burning, blazing, flaming (see barnā, imperf. part. bartā).
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit व्रत (vrata).

def: 1. (or brat, for व्रत ) s. m. A meritorious act, a fast, a vow, a religious rite or penance. 2. (see برتنا) Use. 3. (for वरत्रा ) s. f. A thong, leathern girth, rope: see S̱. برها barhā. 4. (H. pres. part. of برنا) Flaming, blazing.

s.m. Any religious act or obligation enjoined by the gods; a self-chosen or voluntary act, any meritorious act of devotion or austerity, a devout observance, fasting, continence, penance, vow, &c. (syn. upās; but this is more correctly applied to a compulsory abstention from food, as through inability to obtain it, or through sickness; whereas brat or barat has a strictly religious signification).
more: E. n. 1. A disease affecting rice crops. 2. A thong; leathern girth; cable; a rope for drawing water from a well ( lāo ). 3. A mark ( baddhī ); streak; wale.

n. f. S. वृत्ति maintenance, from वृत् to live. 1. Livelihood; stipend; pension; income. 2. Assignment; estate; property; endowment. 3. A grant or endowment for maintenance, or for religious and charitable objects. 4. A custom ( rīt ); right; privilege. 5. Supporters ( jijmān ).
s. f. Livelihood, stipend, pension, income, estate.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit व्रत (vrata).

def: s.f. The procession of the bridegroom (to the house of the bride), marriage procession; the company or attendants at a marriage; multitude, flock, assemblage, troop, crowd; — a suit at cards
more: n. f. S. वरयात्रा, H. bar a bridegroom, āt comes. 1. A multitude; crowd; throng; assembly. Barāt kī barāt chaṛh āī . 2. A marriage procession; the company or attendants at a marriage. 1. Dūlhā gail barāt . Prov. No bridegroom no wedding. 2. Beṭe kā ho byāh to bhāī, na sāthī hai, Nā roshnī, na bāje kī āvāz ātī hai, Māṅ pīchhe mailī chādar ek oṛhe jātī hai, Beṭā banā hai dūlhā, to bāvā barātī hai, Muflis kī yeh barāt chaṛhātī hai muflisī ! On poverty. Nazīr. And if the son a wife should take, no brother, nor Companion boon, nor lights, nor music cheers the mind, Wrapped in a shabby dress the mother walks behind, The son the groom, the father th' only guest, no more. Such the festivities when married are the poor! 3. Wedding day. Āj barāt, kal baṛhār . 4. A suit at cards. barāt meṅ jānā, barāt karnā , v. a. To join in a marriage procession. barāt chaṛh'nā , v. n. To set out (a marriage proc…
s. f. A commission, a warrant, assignment, letter.
s. f. The fourteenth day of the month Shaﻌbān, which is appropriated to the commemoration of ancestors. See شب برات shab-i-barāt (which name the day more commonly bears).
subs: The setting out of the bridegroom in procession (to the house of the bride)
barāt meṅ jānā, v.n. To join in a marriage procession.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi برات (brat /⁠barāt⁠/) (c. 1693), from Sanskrit वरयात्रा (varayātrā), a compound of वर (vará, “suitor, bridegroom, husband”) + यात्रा (yā́trā, “journey”), literally “the groom's journey [to the bride]”.

def: direct plural of بَرات (barāt)
s.f. (?) Land situated in the midst of jungles.
s.m. = bareṭhā, q.v. s.m. Washerman (syn. dhobī); fuller; land of the third quality; a plot of ground on which sugar-cane has been lately grown.
s.m. One who joins a marriage procession, or attends a marriage.
more: n. m. One who joins in a marriage procession. 1. Māṅ, beṭī, gāne-wālī; bāp, pūt, barātī . Prov. Mother and daughter nuptial songsters sole, Father and son the wedding party whole. 2. Barātī chale jāëṅge, kām dūlhā, dulhan hī se paṛegā . Prov. The wedding party feasted and gone, the burden on the bride and bridegroom thrown. (Said of outsiders who are not affected by the course they recommend).
s. m. The company and attendants at a marriage.

def: s.f. Orthography.
oblique plural of بَرات (barāt)
vocative plural of بَرات (barāt)
s.m. Cotton land (cf. badar).
s.m. Part of the wall above the door or entrance; keystone (of an arch, &c.).
more: W. Hin. n. m. H. bār a door, uṭṭhā raised. The key-stone; the wall between the door and the ceiling.

def: s.m. = bādav, q.v.
subs: q.v.s.v. bāḍav s.m. A Brāhman; — submarine fire

def: s.f. A certain bird that feeds on fish.
adj. = bārah-wāṅ, q.v. adj. Twelfth; the twelfth; — bārahweṅ, bārahwe, adv. Twelfthly, in the twelfth place.
s.f. Calculating, estimating, considering beforehand what may be necessary for a feast, &c.; calculation, estimate, budget; pay-abstract
more: s. f. Calculating, estimating, considering before hand what may be necessary for a feast, &c. براوردکرنا bar-āwurd karnā, 1. To strike out (of a muster roll). 2. To calculate, to estimate, to carry to account.
subs: v.t. To strike out (of a muster-roll); to calculate, estimate; to carry forward or to account.

def: part. Brought or carried forward, carried to account; — s.m. Estimate, &c. = bar-āward.
n. m. An estimate; a calculation; budget; bill; pay abstract. bar-āvurd karnā , v. a. To strike out (of a muster-roll); calculate; estimate; carry to account.
Brought or carried forward or over (an account). bar-pā karnā , v. a. To set on foot; begin; carry out; create. bar-pā honā , v. n. To take rise; spring; issue.
s.m.f. Creatures; people; nobles.
more: s. plur. (of برية) Creatures, people, nobles.

def: s.m. Satisfying a want of nature; stool, motion; excrement, human ordure; — coming out or forth to battle; fighting.
more: s. m. 1. Stool, motion, excrement, feces. 2. Fighting.

def: v.n. To be ruined, lost, &c. = bar-bād honā, q.v.
more: adj. P. بر on باد wind. See [اُجارَ 1. Wasted; thrown away; laid waste; destroyed; lost; ruined; undone. 1. Bivī shād, mīyāṅ bar-bād . Mah. Prov. The wife at home sits at her ease, the husband worn and ruined is. 2. Bīvī ke marne se ghar bar-bād ho gayā . 3. Maiṅ ne tere pīchhe apnī javānī barbād kar dī . For you my youth's bloom I have sacrificed. 2. Plundered; ravaged; despoiled; sacked. Dillī har sadī meṅ barbād hotī āī hai . Prov. Every century sees Delhī sacked. barbād kar'nā , v. a. To destroy. See ujāṛnā (1 to 7) and uṛānā (11, 12). barbād honā , v. n. See ujaṛnā (1 to 12).

adj. (Given) to the winds; thrown away, misapplied, lost, wasted, destroyed, laid waste, ruined. برباددينا bar-bād denā or بربادکرنا bar-bād karnā, To give to the winds, to throw away, to lose, to waste, misapply, to ruin, destroy, &c. بربادهونا bar-bād honā, To be thrown away, misapplied, or lost.
subs: v.t. To give to the winds, cast or throw away, lose, waste, squander, dissipate, misapply; to lay waste, ruin, destroy, ravage, plunder, &c. — bar-bād honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be ruined, lost, destroyed, thrown away, wasted, squandered, dissipated, laid waste, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برباد (bar-bād, literally “to the wind”). Compare Punjabi بَرْبَاد (barbād), Bengali বরবাদ (borbad).

def: s.f. Loss, ruin, destruction, waste, laying waste, ravage, &c. (syn. ujāṛ; nāś), destruction of crops (in the passage of troops, &c.).
more: n. f. See اُجارَ (1). Destruction of crops (by passing of troops, etc).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَرْبَادی (barbādī). By surface analysis, بَرْبَاد (barbād) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. (?), (local), North wind.
s.m. = baṛwānal, q.v.s.v. baṛwā.
s.f. Muttering, murmuring, grumbling, chattering; talking (in sleep), raving (in delirium)
more: s. f. Muttering, the light-headed talk of a person in a delirium.
subs: v.n. To mutter, &c. = baṛbaṛānā, and baṛṛānā.

def: adv. Repeatedly, often.
s.f. Muttering, grumbling; chatter, gibberish; light-headed talk; talking incoherently in sleep, raving (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To mutter, to talk gibberish, to blabber, to rave.

def: s.f. Muttering, grumbling; chatter, gibberish; light-headed talk; talking incoherently in sleep, raving (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To mutter, to talk gibberish, to blabber, to rave.

def: v.n. To mutter, murmur, grumble; to chatter, prate, jabber, talk nonsense; to talk in sleep, &c. = baṛṛānā, q.v.
more: v. a. To mutter, grumble, chatter nonsense, talk light-headedly, rave.

def: s.f. = baṛbaṛāhaṭ, q.v. s.f. Muttering, grumbling; chatter, gibberish; light-headed talk; talking incoherently in sleep, raving (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To mutter, to talk gibberish, to blabber, to rave.

def: n. m. lit . A burden-bearer. A porter; carrier; a beast of burden; cart.

n. f. 1. The means of conveyance; transport. 2. Carriage; cart or camel hire; cost of conveyance; freight; transit charges; lading; cargo. bār-bardārī ke jānwar , n. m. Cattle. G. G. more properly, draught cattle. bār-i taraddud , n. m. The burden of disproof or rebutment. bār-i-jahāz , n. m. Ship's cargo; cargo. bār-dār ; A. hāmila , adj. 1. Fruitful; laden with fruit; loaded. 2. Pregnant; with child ( peṭ se ).
s.f. A large kind of goat (from Barbary).
more: n. f. From Barbary. A kind of large goat.
s. f. A large kind of goat (from Barbary).

def: s.m. Mutterer, grumbler, chatterer, raver.
more: part. act. (from بڙبڙانا) A chatterer, a mutterer, a grumbler.

def: adj. See s.v. bar, 'a boon, &c.'
n. m. A chatterer; murmurer; grumbler.

ety: S. वर, or वृद्ध = H. बढ़+वश
def: s.m. Power, might, force, strength, prowess, bravery, vigour, violence.
s.f. A species of snake.
more: n. f. A species of snake.

def: s.m. A kind of bean, Dolichos catjang ( = borā).
s.f. A certain musical instrument, the Persian lute (so called from its resemblance to the breast of a duck or goose).
more: s. f. A kind of harp or lute.

def: — See s.v. بڙ for this and all compounds beginning with the contrac. adj. baṛ, 'great.'
s.m. Trick, deceit, deception; affectation, hypocrisy (syn. bhagal: cf. next).
adj. & s.m. Deceptive, false, hypocritical; affected; — impostor, trickster, cheat, hypocrite; affected person (syn. bhagliyā: cf. next).
s.m. = barćhā, q.v. s.m. Spear, lance (see barćhī)
subs: (or barćhe-) bardār, s.m. Spear, man, lancer ( = barćhait).

def: s.m. Small door, postern; verandah, portico; sun-shade.
s.m. A small spear ( = barćhī, q.v.). s.f. Small spear; spear, lance, javelin, dart
s.m. Spear, lance (see barćhī)
more: s. m. A (long slender) spear, a javelin, dart. برچهابردار or برچهي بردار A spearman.
subs: (or barćhe-) bardār, s.m. Spear, man, lancer ( = barćhait).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit *व्रश्चक (*vraścaka, “one who cuts”), from व्रश्च् (vraśc, “to cut”, root) + -अक (-aka, “-er”).

def: s.m. Spearman, lancer.
wety: From بَرْچھا (barchā) + ـعیط (-ait, “agentive suffix”).

def: s.f. Small spear; spear, lance, javelin, dart
more: n. f. barchhā , n. m. A spear; lance; javelin; dart. barchhī-bardār, barchhait' , n. m. A spearman; a lancer.
s. f. A (long slender) spear, a javelin, dart. برچهابردار or برچهي بردار A spearman.

def: s.f. = barćhī, q.v. s.f. Small spear; spear, lance, javelin, dart
more: s. f. (dakh.) Spear, lance (with a broad head: Harris ). A spear or lance with a wooden stock, carried by faḳīrs ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.m. Cold, coldness, frigidity
subs: s.m. Coldness of the extremities, coldness of death.

def: s.m. Prisoner of war, captive, slave
more: n. m. A slave ( gulām ); captive; prisoner of war. Pūrab kā bardah, uttar kā nīr, Pachchham kā ghoṛā, dakkhan kā chīr . Prov. The East for slaves, the North for sweetest water named, The West for horses, and the South for fabrics famed.
s. m. and f. A captive, a slave. برده فروش barda-farosh, A slave merchant.
subs: s.m. Slave-merchant, slave-dealer; — barda-faroshī, s.f. Selling (one) as a slave; sale of slaves, slave-trade.

def: s.m. = H برڐي बरडी barḍī, s.f. A light, sandy, or gravelly soil.
n. A light, sandy, or stony soil.
n. m. A slave dealer.
n. f. Selling into slavery; sale of imported slaves; the slave trade.
s.f. See s.v. bar, 'boon.'
adj. = bardhmān, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. bar 'boon.'
more: s. m. 1. A wedding gift (to a bride). 2. The answer to a prayer addressed to God or to a saint.

def: n. m. 1. Supplies for an army; provisions; forage. 2. Vessels, bags, etc. in which provisions are kept; the implements or apparatus of a trade; utensils.
adj. See s.v. bar, 'bosom.'
more: n. m. برداشتن to bear up. (In Comp.) A carrier, as huqqā-bar-dār , etc.
part. in compos. (from برداشتن) Taker up, carrier, bearer, holding or raising up. So سونٿي بردار Club-bearer, &c.

ety: act. part. of bar-dāshtan; and = Pehl. burtār: Zend beretar, rt. bar; S. bhartar, rt. bhar
def: adj. & s.m. Holding or raising up; bearer, supporter, carrier, taker up (used in comp. e.g. ḥuqqā-bardār, s.m. The servant who looks after and brings in the ḥuqqā
adj. Fruitful.
s.f. The act of bearing, carrying, &c.; means of carriage or conveyance (used in comp. e.g. bār-bardārī; ḥuqqā-bardārī).
more: s. f. (in compos.) The act of bearing, carrying, &c.

def: s. f. Fruitfulness.
s.f. Endurance, patience, forbearance; stores, supplies; taking goods on credit, credit transaction (syn. ućāpat
more: n. f. 1. Endurance; forbearance; toleration ( sahār ). 2. Taking goods on credit; a credit transaction ( uchāpat ). bardāsht karnā , v. n. Ped. for H. sah'nā , q. v.
(from برداشتن) s. f. Endurance, patience. برداشت کرنا bar-dāsht karnā, To endure, to bear with patience. برداشت هونا bar-dāsht hona, To be tolerable.
subs: (-kī), To endure, suffer, tolerate, bear patiently (syn. sahnā); to look well (after), tend (an animal) well
v.n. To be endured, &c.; to be tolerable.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَرْدَاشْت (bardāšt).

def: part. Raised up, elevated; taken up or away, removed.
more: part. pass. (from برداشتن) Raised up, elevated; removed.

def: to put up with; to tolerate
wety: From بَرْداشْت (bardāśt, “tolerance”) + کَرْنا (karnā, “to do”).

def: s.m. Bullock, &c. ( = barad, q.v.). s.m. Bull, ox, bullock ( = balad).
adj. Increasing, growing; thriving, prosperous; — s.m. A district and city (now called Bardwān).
v.t. To have a cow bulled.
s.f. Drove of laden cattle.
more: s. A drove of cattle loaded.

def: s.f. Papyrus.
s.f. = H برڐا बरडा barḍā, s.m. A light, sandy, or gravelly soil.
n. m. The coldness of death.
part. Sewed; fixed or nailed upon or to (see doḵẖta).
direct masculine plural; oblique masculine singular/plural

conj. (local), For, for the sake (of), &c. = liye, q.v. adv. part. & postpn. Taken, held; holding, having in hand, taking with (him, &c.; — the object precedes liye in the nom. form of the acc., e.g. lāṭhī liye khaṛā hai, 'he stands (there) holding a stick'); — for, for the sake of, on account of, for the purpose of
subs: For this reason, hence; because; to the end (that, -ki)
For this very reason or cause
For what reason? for what? why? wherefore? kis liye ki, For; because, in that; since; inasmuch as.

about, concerning, regarding
s. m. An old man: Sir! (applied to an elderly man).
adv. Once, one time, all at once; at last, at length.
more: adv. 1. Once, one time, all at once. 2. At last, at length.

def: adv. At last, at length; once; however.
more: (dakh.) for P. باري bāre, At last, once, &c.

def: n. m. 1. Superiors; elders; great or rich men. The jackal. "Kāṅdhe dhanush, hāth meṅ bānā, Kahāṅ chale, Dillī sultānā"? The carder. " Ban ke rāo, bikaṭ ke rānā, Baṛen kī bāt baṛe paichhānā ". "With bow and arrow great, O king of Delhī, whither bound"? "O forest king, of armies chief, the great the great hath found." 2. Forefathers; ancestors; predecessors; progenitors. 3. The head of a family; the master of the house. baṛī bahū , n. f. 1. The wife of the elder son. Baṛī bahū ko bulāo, khīr meṅ non ḍāle . Prov. Run for the elder brother's wife, she'll put salt in the khīr (a mess of milk and rice eaten with sugar)! (This proverb is applied as a taunt when a mistake is made by the great or pretentious). 2. A wife who is older than her husband. Baṛī bahū baṛā bhāg . Prov. An elder wife a fortune great. baṛī bī , Fac. baṛī billī , Mah. n. f. A form of address to an elderly or respectable woman. baṛe had kī gintī, pūre had kī gahrī gintī , ( Math .) Integral calculus. baṛī roṭī, baṛī, yā mīṭhī chīz , n. f. Mah. Wom. The Qoran. Galat bilkul paṛhātī hai baṛī roṭī tū Fattū ko; Fazīlat, kyā paṛhī hai de-ke kodoṅ apnī ātū ko! Wom. Jān Sāhab. That best of bread, Koran, you Fattū teach all wrong, You surely paid your teacher in coarse grain, he's taught you wrong. baṛī goṭī' , (Med.) Rinderpest. baṛe miyāṅ , n. m. 1. An old gentleman; the master of the house. Baṛe miyāṅ so baṛe miyāṅ, chhoṭe miyāṅ Subhān Allah! Prov. The old man's well enough, but Lord! the young un out-Herod's Herod. 2. Sir; reverend Sir.
s.m. = bareṭhā, q.v. s.m. Washerman (syn. dhobī); fuller; land of the third quality; a plot of ground on which sugar-cane has been lately grown.

s.m. Wasp ( = barr, baṛ).
s.m. (Maṛwārī), Venerable old man, sage.
s.m. Silk, &c. = abresham, q.v. s.m. Raw silk, silk; sewing silk.
s.f. Negotiating a marriage.
s.f. (?), Name of a tribe of inferior Rājpūts.
s.m. Washerman (syn. dhobī); fuller; land of the third quality; a plot of ground on which sugar-cane has been lately grown.

Tir. n. m. S. वारि water, स्था to stand. i. e. one who stands in the water. 1. A washerman ( dhobī ); fuller. 2. Land of the third quality on which sugarcane has been lately grown. bareṭhan, baraiṭhan , E.; baiṭhāin , Tir. n. f. A washerwoman; laundress ( dhoban ); a washerman's wife.
s.f. Washerwoman, laundress washerman's wife (syn. dhoban).

ety: Pehl. vafr; Zend vafra, rt. vap, 'to throw'
def: s.m. (f. ?), Ice, snow
more: n. f. Z. vafrā . Ice; snow. Taubā hai, Ilāhī! ki paṛī tā ba-kamar barf, Saṛdī se huā jātā hai dil barf, jigar barf . Mukhaīyar. barf, barf sā , adj. As cold as ice. See اولا (1, 2). barf paṛ'nā , v. n. To fall (snow). barf gal'nā , v. n. To be very cold. barf meṅ lagā'nā , v. a. To cool in ice. Lagā-ke barf meṅ sāqī, surāhī-i-maë lā, Jigar kī āg bujhe jis se jald woh shaë lā . Inshā. barfā'nī pahāṛ , n. m. Snowy mountains.
s. m. f. Ice, snow. برف پرورد barf-parward, adj. Iced, cooled in ice.
subs: adj. & s.m. Cooled in ice, iced; — a sherbet, &c. cooled in ice
v.n. To freeze; snow to fall, to snow: barf-sā, adj. (f. -ī), Like ice, cold as ice
(-ko), To put in ice, cool in ice, to ice.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَرْف (barf).

def: adj. Icy; snowy
snow, snowfall
wety: Compound of بَرْف (barf, “ice, snow”) + باری (bārī, “stream, flow, spate”)
